3248.en: Abu al-Malih said: 'They criticize us for writing knowledge down, yet Allah the Most High has said: "Knowledge of it is with my Lord, in a Book." (Surah Ta-Ha, 20:52)'
3248.id: Abu al-Malih berkata: 'Mereka mencela kami karena mencatat ilmu, padahal Allah Ta'ala telah berfirman: "Ilmunya ada di sisi Tuhanku, dalam sebuah Kitab." (Surah Thaha, 20:52)'

3249.en: It was narrated that Sawadah ibn Hayyan heard Mu'awiyah ibn Qurrah Abu Iyas say: 'It used to be said: Whoever does not write down his knowledge, his knowledge will not be counted as knowledge.'
3249.id: Diriwayatkan bahwa Sawadah ibn Hayyan mendengar Mu'awiyah ibn Qurrah Abu Iyas berkata: 'Dahulu dikatakan: Barangsiapa tidak menulis ilmunya, ilmunya tidak akan dianggap sebagai ilmu.'

3250.en: Thumamah ibn 'Abdullah ibn Anas narrated that Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) used to say to his sons: 'O my sons, bind this knowledge by writing it down.'
3250.id: Thumamah ibn 'Abdullah ibn Anas meriwayatkan bahwa Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) biasa berkata kepada anak-anaknya: 'Wahai anak-anakku, ikatlah ilmu ini dengan menuliskannya.'

3251.en: Salm al-'Alawi said: 'I saw Aban writing in the presence of Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) on a wooden tablet.'
3251.id: Salm al-'Alawi berkata: 'Aku melihat Aban menulis di hadapan Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) pada sebuah papan tulis dari kayu.'

3252.en: It was narrated that al-Hasan ibn Jabir asked Abu Umamah al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) about writing down knowledge, and he said: 'There is no harm in that.'
3252.id: Diriwayatkan bahwa al-Hasan ibn Jabir bertanya kepada Abu Umamah al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) tentang penulisan ilmu, maka ia berkata: 'Tidak mengapa melakukan itu.'

3253.en: Bashir ibn Nahik said: 'I used to write down what I heard from Abu Hurayrah (may Allah be pleased with him). When I wanted to part from him, I brought him his book, read it to him, and said: "Is this what I heard from you?" He said: "Yes."'
3253.id: Bashir ibn Nahik berkata: 'Aku biasa mencatat apa yang aku dengar dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu). Ketika aku ingin berpisah darinya, aku datang membawa kitabnya, membacakannya kepadanya, dan berkata: "Apakah ini yang aku dengar darimu?" Ia berkata: "Ya."'

3254.en: Sa'id ibn Jubayr said: 'I used to hear hadith at night from Ibn 'Umar and Ibn 'Abbas (may Allah be pleased with them both), and I would write it on the front part of my camel saddle.'
3254.id: Sa'id ibn Jubayr berkata: 'Aku biasa mendengar hadits di malam hari dari Ibnu 'Umar dan Ibnu 'Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma), lalu aku menuliskannya pada bagian depan pelanaku.'

3255.en: Abdullah ibn 'Amr said: 'Nothing makes me love remaining alive except al-Sadiqah and al-Waht. As for al-Sadiqah, it is a document I wrote from the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him). As for al-Waht, it is a piece of land which 'Amr ibn al-'As (may Allah be pleased with him) gave in charity and used to tend to.'
3255.id: Abdullah ibn 'Amr berkata: 'Tidak ada yang membuat aku ingin tetap hidup kecuali al-Sadiqah dan al-Waht. Adapun al-Sadiqah, ia adalah sebuah dokumen yang aku tulis dari Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wasallam). Adapun al-Waht, ia adalah sebidang tanah yang 'Amr ibn al-'Ash (radhiyallahu 'anhu) sedekahkan dan biasa merawatnya.'

3256.en: It was narrated that 'Amr ibn Abi Sufyan heard 'Umar ibn al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with him) say: 'Bind knowledge by writing it down.'
3256.id: Diriwayatkan bahwa 'Amr ibn Abi Sufyan mendengar 'Umar ibn al-Khattab (radhiyallahu 'anhu) berkata: 'Ikatlah ilmu dengan menuliskannya.'

3257.en: Ibn Jurayj narrated from 'Abd al-Malik ibn 'Abdullah ibn Abi Sufyan al-Thaqafi, from Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with them both), that he said: 'Bind this knowledge by writing it down.'
3257.id: Ibnu Jurayj meriwayatkan dari 'Abd al-Malik ibn 'Abdullah ibn Abi Sufyan al-Thaqafi, dari Ibnu 'Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhuma), bahwa ia berkata: 'Ikatlah ilmu ini dengan menuliskannya.'

3258.en: Sa'id ibn Jubayr said: 'I used to travel with Ibn 'Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) on the road to Makkah at night. He would narrate hadith to me and I would write it on the front part of the camel saddle, then in the morning I would write it out properly.'
3258.id: Sa'id ibn Jubayr berkata: 'Aku biasa bepergian bersama Ibnu 'Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) di jalan menuju Makkah pada malam hari. Ia menyampaikan hadits kepadaku dan aku menuliskannya pada bagian depan pelana unta, kemudian di pagi hari aku menyalinnya dengan baik.'

3259.en: Sa'id ibn Jubayr said: 'I used to write in the presence of Ibn 'Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) on a parchment, and also write on my sandals.'
3259.id: Sa'id ibn Jubayr berkata: 'Aku biasa menulis di hadapan Ibnu 'Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) pada selembar kulit, dan juga menulis di atas sandalku.'

3260.en: Sa'id ibn Jubayr said: 'I used to sit with Ibn 'Abbas and write on the parchment until it was full, then I would flip over my sandals and write on their backs.'
3260.id: Sa'id ibn Jubayr berkata: 'Aku biasa duduk bersama Ibnu 'Abbas dan menulis di atas lembaran hingga penuh, kemudian aku membalik sandalku dan menulis di bagian belakangnya.'

3261.en: 'Ubayd al-Muktib said: 'I saw people writing down the tafsir in the presence of Mujahid.'
3261.id: 'Ubaid al-Muktib berkata: 'Aku melihat orang-orang menuliskan tafsir di hadapan Mujahid.'

3262.en: Abdullah ibn Hanash said: 'I saw people writing in the presence of al-Bara', using the tips of reeds on their palms.'
3262.id: Abdullah ibn Hanash berkata: 'Aku melihat orang-orang menulis di hadapan al-Bara', menggunakan ujung-ujung batang di atas telapak tangan mereka.'

3263.en: It was narrated that Ibn 'Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) narrated a hadith to 'Antarah's father, and he said: 'Shall I write it from you?' He said: 'He gave me permission, though he was reluctant.'
3263.id: Diriwayatkan bahwa Ibnu 'Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) menyampaikan sebuah hadits kepada ayah 'Antarah, lalu ia berkata: 'Bolehkah aku menuliskannya darimu?' Ia berkata: 'Ia memberiku izin, meski dengan enggan.'

3264.en: Raja' ibn Haywah narrated: 'Hisham ibn 'Abd al-Malik wrote to his governor asking him to question me about a hadith. Raja' said: "I would have forgotten it had it not been written down with me."'
3264.id: Raja' ibn Haywah meriwayatkan: 'Hisyam ibn 'Abd al-Malik menulis kepada gubernurnya untuk menanyaiku tentang sebuah hadits. Raja' berkata: "Aku pasti sudah melupakannya seandainya tidak tersimpan dalam tulisanku."'

3265.en: Hisham ibn al-Ghaz said: ''Ata' ibn Abi Rabah used to be asked questions, and whatever he answered was written down in his presence.'
3265.id: Hisyam ibn al-Ghaz berkata: ''Atha' ibn Abi Rabah biasa ditanyai pertanyaan, dan apapun yang ia jawab dituliskan di hadapannya.'

3266.en: It was narrated that Sulayman ibn Musa saw Nafi', the freed slave of Ibn 'Umar, dictating his knowledge while it was being written down in his presence.
3266.id: Diriwayatkan bahwa Sulayman ibn Musa melihat Nafi', bekas budak Ibnu 'Umar, mendiktekan ilmunya sementara itu dituliskan di hadapannya.

3267.en: Al-Mubarak ibn Sa'id said: 'Sufyan used to write hadith at night on the wall, and when morning came he would copy it and then erase it.'
3267.id: Al-Mubarak ibn Sa'id berkata: 'Sufyan biasa menulis hadits di malam hari di dinding, dan ketika pagi tiba ia menyalinnya kemudian menghapusnya.'

3268.en: 'Awn ibn 'Abdullah said: 'I said to 'Umar ibn 'Abd al-'Aziz: "Such-and-such a man, a Companion of the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him), narrated to me..." and 'Umar recognized him. I said: "He narrated to me that the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said: 'Verily, modesty, chastity, and speechlessness of the tongue — not speechlessness of the heart — and fiqh are part of faith, and they increase in the Hereafter while decreasing in this world, and what they increase in the Hereafter is greater. And obscenity, harshness, and miserliness are from hypocrisy, and they increase in this world while decreasing in the Hereafter, and what they decrease in the Hereafter is greater.'" Abu Qilabah said: 'Umar ibn 'Abd al-'Aziz came out to us for Dhuhr prayer carrying a piece of paper, then came out to us for 'Asr prayer still holding it. I said to him: "O Commander of the Faithful, what is this writing?" He said: "This is a hadith that 'Awn ibn 'Abdullah narrated to me, which pleased me, so I wrote it down." And in it was this very hadith.'
3268.id: 'Awn ibn 'Abdullah berkata: 'Aku berkata kepada 'Umar ibn 'Abd al-'Aziz: "Fulan, seorang sahabat Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wasallam), meriwayatkan kepadaku..." dan 'Umar mengenalnya. Aku berkata: "Ia meriwayatkan kepadaku bahwa Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wasallam) bersabda: 'Sesungguhnya rasa malu, kesucian diri, dan kebisuan lisan — bukan kebisuan hati — serta fiqh adalah bagian dari iman, dan semua itu bertambah di akhirat sementara berkurang di dunia, dan pertambahannya di akhirat lebih besar. Sedangkan ucapan kotor, kekerasan, dan kekikiran adalah dari kemunafikan, dan semua itu bertambah di dunia sementara berkurang di akhirat, dan pengurangannya di akhirat lebih besar.'" Abu Qilabah berkata: 'Umar ibn 'Abd al-'Aziz keluar menemui kami untuk shalat Dhuhur membawa selembar kertas, kemudian keluar menemui kami untuk shalat 'Ashar masih membawanya. Aku berkata kepadanya: "Wahai Amirul Mukminin, apa tulisan ini?" Ia berkata: "Ini adalah hadits yang 'Awn ibn 'Abdullah sampaikan kepadaku, yang menakjubkan aku, maka aku menuliskannya." Dan di dalamnya terdapat hadits ini.'

3269.en: Shurahbil ibn Abi Sa'd narrated: 'Al-Hasan (may Allah be pleased with him) called his sons and his brother's sons and said: "O my sons and my brother's sons, you are the youth of a people who are soon to become the elders of others. So learn knowledge, and whoever among you cannot narrate it — or he said: memorize it — let him write it down and keep it in his house."'
3269.id: Shurahbil ibn Abi Sa'd meriwayatkan: 'Al-Hasan (radhiyallahu 'anhu) memanggil anak-anaknya dan anak-anak saudaranya, lalu berkata: "Wahai anak-anakku dan anak-anak saudaraku, kalian adalah generasi muda suatu kaum yang segera akan menjadi para tetua kaum yang lain. Maka pelajarilah ilmu, dan barangsiapa di antara kalian yang tidak mampu meriwayatkannya — atau ia berkata: menghafalnya — maka hendaklah ia menuliskannya dan menyimpannya di rumahnya."'

3270.en: Jarir (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said: 'Whoever establishes a good practice that is followed after him will have a reward like the reward of those who act upon it, without their rewards being diminished in any way. And whoever establishes an evil practice, he will bear a burden like the burden of those who act upon it, without their burdens being diminished in any way.'
3270.id: Jarir (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melaporkan bahwa Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wasallam) bersabda: 'Barangsiapa menetapkan sunnah (kebiasaan) yang baik yang diikuti setelahnya, ia akan mendapat pahala seperti pahala orang-orang yang mengamalkannya, tanpa pahala mereka berkurang sedikit pun. Dan barangsiapa menetapkan sunnah yang buruk, ia akan menanggung beban seperti beban orang-orang yang mengamalkannya, tanpa beban mereka berkurang sedikit pun.'

3271.en: Abu Hurayrah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said: 'Whoever calls to guidance will have a reward like the rewards of those who follow him, without their rewards being diminished in any way. And whoever calls to misguidance will bear a sin like the sins of those who follow him, without their sins being diminished in any way.'
3271.id: Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melaporkan bahwa Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wasallam) bersabda: 'Barangsiapa menyeru kepada petunjuk, ia akan mendapat pahala seperti pahala orang-orang yang mengikutinya, tanpa pahala mereka berkurang sedikit pun. Dan barangsiapa menyeru kepada kesesatan, ia akan menanggung dosa seperti dosa orang-orang yang mengikutinya, tanpa dosa mereka berkurang sedikit pun.'

3272.en: Jarir ibn 'Abdullah (may Allah be pleased with him) narrated: 'The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) delivered a sermon to us, urging the people to give charity. They were slow to respond until anger showed on his face. Then a man from the Ansar came with a bag of coins, and the people followed in succession until joy was seen on his face. He said: "Whoever establishes a good practice will have its reward and the like of the reward of all who act upon it, without their rewards being diminished in any way. And whoever establishes an evil practice will bear its burden and the like of the burden of all who act upon it, without their burdens being diminished in any way."'
3272.id: Jarir ibn 'Abdullah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) meriwayatkan: 'Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wasallam) berkhutbah kepada kami, mendorong orang-orang untuk bersedekah. Mereka lambat merespons hingga tampak kemarahan di wajahnya. Kemudian seorang laki-laki dari kaum Anshar datang membawa sekantong koin, dan orang-orang pun saling menyusul hingga terlihat kegembiraan di wajahnya. Ia bersabda: "Barangsiapa menetapkan kebiasaan yang baik, ia akan mendapat pahalanya dan yang semisal pahala semua orang yang mengamalkannya, tanpa pahala mereka berkurang sedikit pun. Dan barangsiapa menetapkan kebiasaan yang buruk, ia akan menanggung bebannya dan yang semisal beban semua orang yang mengamalkannya, tanpa beban mereka berkurang sedikit pun."'

3273.en: Hassan ibn 'Atiyyah narrated that the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said: 'I am the greatest of you in reward on the Day of Resurrection, because I have my own reward and the like of the reward of all who follow me.'
3273.id: Hassan ibn 'Atiyyah meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wasallam) bersabda: 'Aku adalah yang paling besar di antara kalian pahalanya pada hari kiamat, karena aku memiliki pahalaku sendiri ditambah yang semisal pahala semua orang yang mengikutiku.'

3274.en: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said: 'Whoever calls to a matter — even if only one man calls another man — will be held accountable for it on the Day of Resurrection, attached to his withers.' Then he recited: 'And stop them, for they will be questioned.' (Surah al-Saffat, 37:24)
3274.id: Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melaporkan bahwa Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wasallam) bersabda: 'Barangsiapa menyeru kepada suatu perkara — meskipun hanya seorang laki-laki menyeru laki-laki lain — ia akan dimintai pertanggungjawaban atasnya pada hari kiamat, melekat pada bahunya.' Kemudian beliau membaca: 'Dan tahanlah mereka, karena sesungguhnya mereka akan ditanya.' (Surah al-Shaffat, 37:24)'

3275.en: Ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) said: 'Four things are given to a man after his death: a third of his wealth, if he was obedient to Allah in regard to it before that; a righteous child who supplicates for him after his death; a good practice which the man established and which is acted upon after his death; and a hundred people who intercede for a man — they are granted their intercession for him.'
3275.id: Ibnu Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) berkata: 'Empat hal diberikan kepada seseorang setelah kematiannya: sepertiga hartanya, jika ia sebelumnya taat kepada Allah dalam hal itu; anak yang shalih yang mendoakannya setelah kematiannya; kebiasaan yang baik yang ia tetapkan dan diamalkan setelah kematiannya; dan seratus orang yang memberikan syafaat bagi seseorang — mereka dikabulkan syafaatnya untuknya.'

3276.en: Al-A'mash said: 'We persistently tried to get Ibrahim (al-Nakha'i) to sit leaning against a pillar, but he refused.'
3276.id: Al-A'mash berkata: 'Kami terus-menerus berusaha membuat Ibrahim (al-Nakha'i) duduk bersandar pada tiang, namun ia menolak.'

3277.en: It was narrated from al-Mughirah, from Ibrahim (al-Nakha'i), that he used to dislike leaning against a pillar.
3277.id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Mughirah, dari Ibrahim (al-Nakha'i), bahwa ia tidak menyukai bersandar pada tiang.

3278.en: Al-Mughirah said: 'Ibrahim (al-Nakha'i) would not begin speaking (about hadith) until he was asked.'
3278.id: Al-Mughirah berkata: 'Ibrahim (al-Nakha'i) tidak akan memulai berbicara (tentang hadits) hingga ditanya.'

3279.en: Khaythamah said: 'Al-Harith ibn Qays al-Ju'fi was a companion of 'Abdullah (ibn Mas'ud), and they used to admire him. He would sit with one or two men and narrate hadith to them, but when more people gathered, he would get up and leave them.'
3279.id: Khaythamah berkata: 'Al-Harith ibn Qays al-Ju'fi adalah seorang sahabat 'Abdullah (ibn Mas'ud), dan mereka biasa mengaguminya. Ia biasa duduk bersama satu atau dua orang dan menyampaikan hadits kepada mereka, namun ketika banyak orang berkumpul, ia bangkit dan meninggalkan mereka.'

3280.en: Alqamah said: 'When 'Abdullah (ibn Mas'ud, may Allah be pleased with him) died, it was said to him: "Will you not sit and teach people the Sunnah?" He said: "Do you want people to trample on my heels?"'
3280.id: Alqamah berkata: 'Ketika 'Abdullah (ibn Mas'ud, radhiyallahu 'anhu) wafat, dikatakan kepadanya: "Tidakkah engkau duduk dan mengajarkan Sunnah kepada manusia?" Ia berkata: "Apakah kalian ingin orang-orang menginjak tumit-tumitku?"'

3281.en: Sulaym ibn Handhalah narrated: 'We came to Ubayy ibn Ka'b to sit with him. When he stood up, we stood up and were walking behind him. 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) caught up with us. He followed and struck him with his whip. He shielded himself with his forearms and said: "O Commander of the Faithful, what should we do?" He said: "Do you not see? It is a trial for the one followed and a humiliation for the follower."'
3281.id: Sulaym ibn Handhalah meriwayatkan: 'Kami datang kepada Ubay ibn Ka'b untuk duduk bersamanya. Ketika ia berdiri, kami berdiri dan berjalan di belakangnya. 'Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) menyusul kami. Ia mengikutinya dan memukulnya dengan cambuknya. Ia melindungi dirinya dengan kedua lengannya dan berkata: "Wahai Amirul Mukminin, apa yang harus kami lakukan?" Ia berkata: "Tidakkah kalian melihat? Itu adalah fitnah bagi yang diikuti dan kehinaan bagi yang mengikuti."'

3282.en: Ibrahim (al-Nakha'i) said: 'They used to dislike having their heels stepped on (i.e., being followed in a crowd).'
3282.id: Ibrahim (al-Nakha'i) berkata: 'Mereka biasa tidak menyukai ditinggali bekas tapak kaki di tumit mereka (yaitu, diikuti dalam kerumunan).'

3283.en: It was narrated that when a man walked alongside Muhammad ibn Sirin (may Allah have mercy on him), he would stop and say: 'Do you have a need? If he had a need, he would fulfill it. But if the man continued walking with him, he would stop again and say: "Do you have a need?"'
3283.id: Diriwayatkan bahwa ketika seseorang berjalan bersama Muhammad ibn Sirin (rahimahullah), ia akan berhenti dan berkata: 'Apakah kamu memiliki keperluan? Jika ia memiliki keperluan, ia memenuhinya. Namun jika orang itu terus berjalan bersamanya, ia akan berhenti lagi dan berkata: "Apakah kamu memiliki keperluan?"'

3284.en: Ibrahim (al-Nakha'i) said: 'Beware of having your heels stepped on (i.e., being followed in crowds).'
3284.id: Ibrahim (al-Nakha'i) berkata: 'Jauhilah oleh kalian diinjak tumit-tumit kalian (yaitu, diikuti dalam keramaian).'

3285.en: 'Asim ibn Damrah saw people following Sa'id ibn Jubayr. He said — and I believe he said — that he forbade them and said: 'This action of yours, or this walking of yours, is a humiliation for the follower and a trial for the one followed.'
3285.id: 'Ashim ibn Dhamrah melihat orang-orang mengikuti Sa'id ibn Jubayr. Ia berkata — dan aku kira ia berkata — bahwa ia melarang mereka dan berkata: 'Perbuatan kalian ini, atau cara berjalan kalian ini, adalah kehinaan bagi yang mengikuti dan fitnah bagi yang diikuti.'

3286.en: Ibn 'Awn narrated: 'I consulted Muhammad (ibn Sirin, may Allah have mercy on him) about a building I wanted to construct in al-Kalla'. He gave me advice, and said: "When you want to lay the foundation, inform me so I can come with you." I came to him, and while we were walking, a man came and walked with him. He stopped and said: "Do you have a need?" The man said: "No." He said: "Well then, go." Then he turned to me and said: "You too, go." He said: "So I left until I took a different road."'
3286.id: Ibnu 'Awn meriwayatkan: 'Aku berkonsultasi dengan Muhammad (ibn Sirin, rahimahullah) tentang sebuah bangunan yang ingin aku bangun di al-Kalla'. Ia memberiku saran, dan berkata: "Ketika kamu ingin meletakkan pondasinya, beritahu aku agar aku bisa datang bersamamu." Aku mendatanginya, dan saat kami sedang berjalan, seorang laki-laki datang dan berjalan bersamanya. Ia berhenti dan berkata: "Apakah kamu memiliki keperluan?" Laki-laki itu berkata: "Tidak." Ia berkata: "Kalau begitu, pergilah." Kemudian ia berbalik kepadaku dan berkata: "Kamu juga, pergi." Ia berkata: "Maka aku pun pergi hingga aku mengambil jalan yang berbeda."'

3287.en: It was narrated that al-Rabi' (ibn Khuthaym), when people came to him, used to say: 'I seek refuge in Allah from your evil' — meaning, from his companions.
3287.id: Diriwayatkan bahwa al-Rabi' (ibn Khuthaym), ketika orang-orang datang kepadanya, biasa berkata: 'Aku berlindung kepada Allah dari keburukan kalian' — maksudnya, dari para sahabatnya.

3288.en: 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Bishr said: 'We were with Khabbab ibn al-Aratt (may Allah be pleased with him), and his companions gathered around him while he was silent. It was said to him: "Will you not narrate to your companions?" He said: "I fear that I would say to them what I do not practice myself."'
3288.id: 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Bishr berkata: 'Kami berada bersama Khabbab ibn al-Aratt (radhiyallahu 'anhu), dan para sahabatnya berkumpul di sekelilingnya sementara ia diam. Dikatakan kepadanya: "Tidakkah engkau mau bercerita kepada para sahabatmu?" Ia berkata: "Aku takut jika aku mengatakan kepada mereka apa yang tidak aku amalkan sendiri."'

3289.en: Salih narrated: 'I heard al-Sha'bi say: "I wish I could come out of my deeds even — nothing for me and nothing against me."'
3289.id: Shalih meriwayatkan: 'Aku mendengar al-Sya'bi berkata: "Aku berharap aku bisa keluar dari amal perbuatanku dengan seimbang — tidak ada untukku dan tidak ada yang melawanku."'

3290.en: It was narrated from al-Hasan that Ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) was walking while people stepped on his heels. He said: 'Do not tread on my heels. By Allah, if you knew what goes on behind my closed door, not one of you would follow me.'
3290.id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Hasan bahwa Ibnu Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) sedang berjalan sementara orang-orang menginjak tumitnya. Ia berkata: 'Jangan injak tumitku. Demi Allah, jika kalian mengetahui apa yang terjadi di balik pintu yang aku tutup, tidak seorang pun di antara kalian yang akan mengikutiku.'

3291.en: Sa'id ibn Jubayr said: 'It is a trial for the one followed and a humiliation for the follower.'
3291.id: Sa'id ibn Jubayr berkata: 'Itu adalah fitnah bagi yang diikuti dan kehinaan bagi yang mengikuti.'

3292.en: It was narrated that people were walking behind 'Ali (may Allah be pleased with him), and he said: 'Keep the sound of your sandals away from me, for it corrupts the hearts of foolish men.'
3292.id: Diriwayatkan bahwa orang-orang berjalan di belakang 'Ali (radhiyallahu 'anhu), maka ia berkata: 'Jauhkan suara sandal-sandal kalian dariku, karena itu merusak hati orang-orang yang lemah akalnya.'

3293.en: Yazid ibn Hazim narrated: 'I heard al-Hasan say: "Verily, the sound of sandals around men seldom fails to leave a mark on foolish ones."'
3293.id: Yazid ibn Hazim meriwayatkan: 'Aku mendengar al-Hasan berkata: "Sesungguhnya suara sandal di sekitar orang-orang jarang gagal meninggalkan kesan pada orang-orang yang bodoh."'

3294.en: It was narrated from Laith, from Tawus: 'When one or two men sat with him, he would get up and move away.'
3294.id: Diriwayatkan dari Laits, dari Thawus: 'Ketika satu atau dua orang duduk bersamanya, ia bangkit dan menjauh.'

3295.en: Abu Barzah al-Aslami (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said: 'The feet of a servant will not move on the Day of Resurrection until he is asked about his life and how he spent it, about his knowledge and what he did with it, about his wealth — where he acquired it and how he spent it, and about his body and how he wore it out.'
3295.id: Abu Barzah al-Aslami (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melaporkan bahwa Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wasallam) bersabda: 'Kedua kaki seorang hamba tidak akan bergerak pada hari kiamat hingga ia ditanya tentang umurnya dan bagaimana ia menghabiskannya, tentang ilmunya dan apa yang ia perbuat dengannya, tentang hartanya — dari mana ia memperolehnya dan bagaimana ia membelanjakannya, dan tentang tubuhnya dan bagaimana ia menggunakannya.'

3296.en: Mu'adh ibn Jabal (may Allah be pleased with him) said: 'Allah will not leave the servants on the Day of Resurrection — the Day when people stand before the Lord of all worlds — until He questions them about four things: how they spent their lifetimes, how they wore out their bodies, what they earned and how they spent it, and what they did with what they knew.'
3296.id: Mu'adz ibn Jabal (radhiyallahu 'anhu) berkata: 'Allah tidak akan membiarkan para hamba pada hari kiamat — hari ketika manusia berdiri di hadapan Tuhan semesta alam — hingga Dia menanyai mereka tentang empat hal: bagaimana mereka menghabiskan umur mereka, bagaimana mereka menggunakan tubuh mereka, apa yang mereka peroleh dan bagaimana mereka membelanjakannya, dan apa yang mereka perbuat dengan apa yang mereka ketahui.'

3297.en: Mu'adh ibn Jabal (may Allah be pleased with him) said: 'The feet of a servant will not move on the Day of Resurrection until he is asked about four things: his life and how he spent it, his body and how he wore it out, his wealth — where he acquired it and where he placed it, and his knowledge — what he did with it.'
3297.id: Mu'adz ibn Jabal (radhiyallahu 'anhu) berkata: 'Kedua kaki seorang hamba tidak akan bergerak pada hari kiamat hingga ia ditanya tentang empat hal: umurnya dan bagaimana ia menghabiskannya, tubuhnya dan bagaimana ia menggunakannya, hartanya — dari mana ia memperolehnya dan di mana ia menempatkannya, dan ilmunya — apa yang ia perbuat dengannya.'
